pattern: In a functional graph each start reaches a simple chain (until it hits a cycle), so walking from both starts records two distance arrays and the answer minimizes the larger of the two over commonly reachable nodes.
hints: Each node has at most one successor, so the set of nodes reachable from a start is just the path you get by following edges until you repeat or run out.
Record the distance from node1 to every reachable node, and likewise from node2.
Over all nodes reachable from both, minimize max(d1, d2), breaking ties by smallest index.

edge_inputs.description: one start is the meeting node itself
graph contains a cycle that must not loop forever
tie on max distance resolved by smaller index

stress.description: random functional graph with cycles and dead ends
stress.note: edges[i] != i enforced; cycles exercised by random targets
